data.title: Arcadium Lithium gains amid China filing on Rio Tinto deal
data.description:
Arcadium Lithium (NYSE:ALTM) rose 1.7% after a filing in China related to the lithium company's planned $6.7 billion to Rio Tinto (RIO).

The transaction was filed under "simplified procedure" to a regional office of the company's main antitrust authority, according to a posting on Chongqing Administration for Market Regulation's website that was circulating among traders on Friday.

The Arcadium (ALTM) deal has raised some potential national security worries in the U.S. as Aluminum Corp of China (OTCPK:ALMMF) holds a 14.99% stake in Rio Tinto (RIO). Lithium is a key ingredient in rechargeable batteries, including those for electric vehicles.

data.title: DocuSign surges after 'strong' Q3 keeps analysts largely positive
data.description:
DocuSign's (NASDAQ:DOCU) stock soared about 21% on Friday after third quarter results and fiscal 2025 guidance exceeded expectations, while analysts held largely positive views on the company.

RBC Capital Markets kept its Sector Perform rating and raised the price target on the shares to $90 from $57.

Analysts led by Rishi Jaluria said that DocuSign reported a good beat-and-raise quarter, leading shares to rise after-hours. Billings acceleration highlighted the quarter. One-third was steered by early renewals, but usage trends are increasing, they added.

The analysts noted that guidance was raised well ahead of outperformance, and Net Revenue Retention, or NRR, improved. While there was improvement in the core business, the case for meaningful acceleration rests on ramping its Intelligent Agreement Management, or IAM, platform, which remains early, according to the analysts.

Jaluria and his team said that while they acknowledge the quarter was a step in the right direction, the risk/reward remains balanced given that shares have significantly re-rated.

Citi maintained its Buy rating and increased its price target on the shares to $113, while noting that DocuSign re-accelerated growth, and that there are additional tailwinds ahead.

The analysts said DocuSign posted a strong third quarter beat-and-raise across the board, delivering a five point billings beat along with re-accelerating subscription revenue and total revenue.

While early renewals were a partial factor, the company saw resilient customer addition metrics (300K, and total), an uptick in NRR to 100%, and IAM upside. The analysts added that they remain encouraged by various growth signals, such as — top-of-funnel trends look healthy with customer growth of over 11% year-over-year, envelope utilization improvement, international momentum (+14% year-over-year) along with further IAM enablement which could provide some upside to the fourth quarter.

In addition, Citi noted that it is encouraged by expense discipline, with a beat and raise on both gross margin and operating margin and believes the company could see improvement (though measured) in fiscal 2026-2027.

The analysts said they kept the Buy rating as they see potential for continued international expansion, IAM optionality in fiscal 2026, and a cyclical recovery in consumption.

Related stocks: Adobe (ADBE) +2% on Friday.

data.title: Growth-at-a-reasonable-price Stocks according to Goldman: FLNC, ENPH, AMD
data.description:
Goldman Sachs analysts listed “laggards” that offer high topline growth at reasonable valuations.

These are names that are expected to see at least 10% sales growth for their 2024-2026 estimated CAGR and low price-earnings-to-growth ratio.

They are all buy-rated stocks with at least 10% upside to their price target, and with sales growth greater than 10% and price-earnings-to-growth ratio less than 1.

“We look for stocks that are poised to grow into their multiples,” wrote Deep Mehta, vice president at Goldman Sachs, in a tactical research note.

Here are the growth opportunities at reasonable valuations:

  • Fluence Energy Inc. (FLNC) – Sales growth year-over-year CAGR 24’-26’: 36%
  • Enphase Energy Inc. (ENPH) – Sales growth year-over-year CAGR 24’-26’: 33%
  • Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) – Sales growth year-over-year CAGR 24’-26’: 29%
  • Teradyne Inc. (TER) – Sales growth year-over-year CAGR 24’-26’: 24%
  • Remitly Global Inc. (RELY) – Sales growth year-over-year CAGR 24’-26’: 23%
  • MediaAlpha Inc. (MAX) – Sales growth year-over-year CAGR 24’-26’: 19%
  • Workiva Inc. (WK) – Sales growth year-over-year CAGR 24’-26’: 18%
  • Shoals Technologies (SHLS) – Sales growth year-over-year CAGR 24’-26’: 15%
  • Pinterest Inc. (PINS) – Sales growth year-over-year CAGR 24’-26’: 15%
  • Array Technologies Inc. (ARRY) – Sales growth year-over-year CAGR 24’-26’: 15%
  • Entegris Inc. (ENTG) – Sales growth year-over-year CAGR 24’-26’: 12%

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow tick higher after latest payrolls print arrives
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages pushed up as markets received the latest November's non-farm payrolls data.

Early on and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.3%, the S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.2%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) dipped 4 basis points to 4.13%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 6 basis points at 4.08%.

On the economic front, November nonfarm payrolls rebounded from October's print. The November nonfarm payrolls data came in at +227K versus the +211K consensus level.

At the same time the unemployment rate tagged +4.2% which was in-line with forecasts and an increase from October's +4.1% level.

A majority of traders are betting on a 25-basis-point rate cut this month to 4.25%-4.50%, according to CME's FedWatch tool.

Investor will also closely watch commentary from Fed officials slated to speak on Friday - Governor Michelle Bowman, Chicago Fed's Austan Goolsbee, Cleveland Fed's Beth Hammack and San Francisco Fed's Mary Daly.

Other economic reports scheduled for Friday include December's consumer sentiment index (consensus 73.1) and October's consumer credit data (consensus $10.10B).

Among active stocks, DocuSign (DOCU) soared 20% and Ulta Beauty (ULTA) rose 12.6% on strong results.

In the crypto market, bitcoin (BTC-USD) is continuing to trade below $100,000 as it hovers near $98.9K.

data.title: AIG commences cash tender offers for up to $750M outstanding notes
data.description:
• Insurance company American International Group (NYSE:AIG) has commenced cash tender offers for up to $750M worth of outstanding notes.
  • The company has initiated 12 separate offers.
  • The tender offers involve 8.175% Series A-6 Junior Subordinated Debentures Due 2058, 6.820% Notes Due 2037, 6.250% Notes Due 2036, 6.250% Series A-1 Junior Subordinated Debentures Due 2037, 5.750% Series A-9 Junior Subordinated Debentures Due 2048, 4.800% Notes Due 2045, 4.750% Notes Due 2048, 4.700% Notes Due 2035, 4.500% Notes Due 2044, 4.375% Notes Due 2055, 4.250% Notes Due 2029, and 4.200% Notes Due 2028.
  • The offers are expected to expire on December 12 5:00 PM ET.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: BAE Systems awarded $2.5B in contracts with Denmark and Sweden for new CV90 combat vehicles
data.description:
• BAE Systems (OTCPK:BAESF) said on Friday that it has signed individual contracts worth a total of approximately $2.5B with both Sweden and Denmark for new CV90 combat vehicles.
  • These contracts come under a new three-party framework agreement with the Danish Ministry of Defence Acquisition and Logistics Organisation and the Swedish Defense Materiel Administration to expand the acquisition of new CV9035MkIIIC combat vehicles for both countries.
  • This builds on an initial contract signed between BAE Systems and Sweden's FMV in May 2024.
  • Under these contracts, Denmark is procuring 115 new CV9035MkIIICs, while Sweden has agreed to acquire a total of 50 new vehicles. The agreement also includes further vehicles for Ukraine financed by the two governments.
  • Together, this takes the total value of CV9035MkIIICs contracted under the framework agreement to approximately $2.5 billion, including spares, support, logistics, and training.

data.title: Vanguard's 2025 economic outlook: Rate cuts, steady growth, and moderating inflation
data.description:
Vanguard has come out and provided its economic year-end outlook along with its 2025 projections as it pertains to interest rates, GDP growth, inflation levels, and unemployment forecasts.

Interest Rates: As it pertains to rates, the ETF issuer stated that it anticipates another rate cut by the FOMC on December 18 which will take the policy rate down to 4.25% - 4.50%. Longer term into 2025 and Vanguard said: “We anticipate the Fed will reduce its rate target further in 2025 to a range of 3.75%–4%.”

GDP: The financial firm believes full year GDP growth will stick at 2.3% for the full year 2024 and the 2025 year will also see growth remain above the 2% threshold.

Inflation: Vanguard noted that it believes core PCE will rise to 2.9% by year-end but believes the levels will come down to 2.5% by the end of 2025.

Unemployment: As in pertains to unemployment, Vanguard forecasts that the levels will “marginally” increase in 2025 to the mid 4% range. For reference, Thursday’s payrolls print showed unemployment tick up to 4.2% in November from 4.1% in October.

For investors looking to keep a pulse on the overall marketplace, they may choose to further analyze index tracking exchange traded funds.

Market Tracking ETFs: (NYSEARCA:DIA), (DDM), (UDOW), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (NASDAQ:QQQ), (QQQM), (QLD), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).
